notes: Pinned OCI SDK contract: the provider resource exposes CreateProject, GetProject, ChangeProjectCompartment, UpdateProject, and DeleteProject, while the paired datasource oci_ai_language_projects exposes ListProjects filters compartment_id, display_name, id, and state and returns project_collection.items from the SDK Items list. In v65.110.0 the service SDK still uses projectId as the tracked-identity field on GetProject, UpdateProject, DeleteProject, and optional ListProjects filtering, and GetWorkRequest reports CREATE_PROJECT, UPDATE_PROJECT, and DELETE_PROJECT operation types plus CREATED, UPDATED, DELETED, IN_PROGRESS, and RELATED resource actions.

Pinned provider implementation: create, update, and delete are work-request-backed, and compartment changes call ChangeProjectCompartment plus WaitForUpdatedState before UpdateProject. The reviewed runtime keeps using service-sdk GetWorkRequest polling and shared async status instead of provider helper waiters.

Repo-authored bind-versus-create stays explicit: when no OCI identifier is tracked, the runtime uses paginated ListProjects matches on compartmentId, displayName, and optional tracked projectId, ignores DELETED summaries, and reuses the same request shape for delete fallback when GetProject returns NotFound.

Repo-authored mutation policy stays explicit: only displayName, description, freeformTags, and definedTags reconcile in place. The runtime now tracks create, update, and delete through GetWorkRequest plus status.async.current, while provider support for changing compartment_id remains out-of-scope drift because the published runtime treats compartmentId as replacement-only and never calls ChangeProjectCompartment.
